Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"As a carpenter from Rome juggling between woodworking projects and family life, I’ve seen the challenges of navigating genuine information online. With stable mental health and limited social media use—dedicating only a few hours monthly—I value authentic connections over noise. My hobbies like photography remind me that details matter; applying this lens to digital content, I encourage skepticism without cynicism. Cross-checking sources as one verifies wood grain patterns ensures integrity. Curating feeds with trusted outlets parallels selecting quality materials for my craft. By setting boundaries on social media use and fostering offline relationships—be it discussing football (calcio) with friends or volunteering—I minimize exposure to misinformation. Engaging in informed discussions at community gatherings, akin to perfecting a wooden piece, requires patience and discernment. Together, proactive verification and critical conversations act as tools against fake content, safeguarding both personal peace and societal discourse. Remember, just as my craft thrives on truth and skill, so should our digital interactions.","reasoning_summary":"User is an educated, balanced individual with moderate conscientiousness, low extraversion, but strong agreeableness and neuroticism. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a carpenter from Rome juggling between woodworking projects and family life, I’ve seen the challenges of navigating genuine information online. With stable mental health and limited social media use—dedicating only a few hours monthly—I value authentic connections over noise. My hobbies like photography remind me that details matter; applying this lens to digital content, I encourage skepticism without cynicism. Cross-checking sources as one verifies wood grain patterns ensures integrity. Curating feeds with trusted outlets parallels selecting quality materials for my craft. By setting boundaries on social media use and fostering offline relationships—be it discussing football (calcio) with friends or volunteering—I minimize exposure to misinformation. Engaging in informed discussions at community gatherings, akin to perfecting a wooden piece, requires patience and discernment. Together, proactive verification and critical conversations act as tools against fake content, safeguarding both personal peace and societal discourse. Remember, just as my craft thrives on truth and skill, so should our digital interactions.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: User is an educated, balanced individual with moderate conscientiousness, low extraversion, but strong agreeableness and neuroticism. Their hobbies suggest appreciation for authenticity akin to craftsmanship. Limited social media use allows focus on verification methods paralleling carpentry precision. Emphasizes community dialogue over solitary research. Advocates structured skepticism informed by personal values of mental wellness and trust.
citations: Nimmo, J. & Swaner, T. (2018). Misinformation Management. Journal of Media Studies.
Carroll, L. (2020). Digital Literacy: Lessons from Craftsmanship. EdTech Magazine
